justification: All 6 required rules identified (plus a 7th reinforcing license gating). Regulatory rules (license verification BR-1/BR-7, record visibility BR-5, note lock BR-6) correctly marked invariant: true. Cancellation rules correctly split by role (BR-3 patient, BR-4 admin) with invariant: false reflecting their policy nature. 13 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, far exceeding the 4 minimum, covering all required scenarios: licensed physician booking (AC-1/AC-2), double-booking (AC-3/AC-4), late cancellation (AC-5/AC-6/AC-7), and unauthorized record access (AC-8/AC-9/AC-10). Confidence level 82 is within the 65-90 range, with clearly stated assumptions about license verification meaning and boundary semantics.

observations.text: All 6 rubric rules identified with correct invariant classification; regulatory vs operational distinction is systematic and well-justified
13 acceptance criteria with precise boundary cases (e.g., AC-4 adjacent slots, AC-13 exact 48-hour boundary)
Role-based cancellation correctly split into two rules (BR-3 patient, BR-4 admin) with appropriate invariant: false classification
Confidence assumptions explicitly note uncertainty about license verification lifecycle and boundary semantics
Rich edge cases covering concurrent booking, license revocation, and boundary timing scenarios

BR-3/BR-4 cancellation rules both marked invariant: false — the role boundary itself (patient cannot cancel after window, only admin can) could be argued as invariant: true per the rubric note, though the classification is defensible as policy
